codeReview.summary: Single new file (system/test/tests/unicode.rs, 652 lines) adds 22 well-structured tests covering all three VAL-UNICODE assertions plus glyph cache expansion. No implementation changes were needed — the existing pipeline already handles Unicode correctly, and these tests prove it. All 22 tests pass, all 1415 total tests pass, release build clean. Two minor non-blocking issues found.

issues.description: Unused constants NUNITO_SANS (line 11) and SOURCE_CODE_PRO (line 13) are defined via include_bytes! but never referenced in any test. Only the _VARIABLE variants are used. This generates 2 compiler warnings. Should be removed for clean builds.

Test unicode_supplementary_glyph_id_truncation_safety cannot actually detect truncation bugs. It checks `gid == 0 || gid < u16::MAX` where gid is already a u16 (truncated from u32 inside shape() at lib.rs:80). If a font had glyph ID > 65535, the truncation would silently produce a wrong u16 value that this test would accept. The test documents the concern but is technically vacuous — to truly test truncation safety, it would need access to the pre-truncation u32 value from HarfRust. Acceptable as documentation-as-test but should be noted.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md says 'Shaping tests go in shaping.rs. Drawing/rasterizer tests go in drawing.rs. Scene graph tests go in scene.rs. Create new test files when the existing ones would become unwieldy.' The worker created unicode.rs for cross-cutting tests spanning shaping, scene graph, and cache. This is a reasonable organizational choice for feature-scoped tests, but the convention in AGENTS.md doesn't mention cross-cutting test files. Consider adding guidance like: 'Cross-cutting feature tests that span multiple subsystems may use a dedicated file named for the feature (e.g., unicode.rs).'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md §Testing & Validation Guidance vs system/test/tests/unicode.rs which imports from scene::*, shaping::*, and drawing::*

summary: PASS. 22 tests in a single new file (unicode.rs) thoroughly cover all three VAL-UNICODE assertions and all four expected behaviors. VAL-UNICODE-001: 8 tests verify Latin Extended codepoints (é, ñ, ü, café) shape with valid glyph IDs and rasterize with non-zero coverage, including fallback chain and glyph cache paths. VAL-UNICODE-002: 3 tests verify 'naïve résumé' round-trips through scene graph (single buffer, double buffer, mixed ASCII/Extended). VAL-UNICODE-003: 8 tests verify supplementary plane codepoints (U+1F600, U+10000, U+10FFFD) don't crash and subsequent ASCII text shapes/rasterizes correctly. 3 additional glyph cache tests confirm the expanded codepoint space is handled. No implementation changes were required — the pipeline already supported Unicode. Two non-blocking issues: unused font constants generating warnings, and a truncation safety test that can't detect the bug it describes. Worker followed the skill procedure correctly and the handoff is accurate.
